Title: Magazine Cover Editorial Beauty Portrait
Teaser: Turns a face and an outfit into a glossy magazine-cover moment — beauty-dish light, flawless skin texture, a confident gaze straight down the lens and clean negative space at the top where a masthead would live. The single most reusable fashion-editorial shot.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT] (or attach a photo), name the [OUTFIT/LOOK], and optionally set the [MOOD] and [BACKDROP COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a fashion-magazine cover photographer and beauty retoucher shooting the lead image for an issue. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] wearing [OUTFIT/LOOK] as a polished editorial beauty portrait with cover-ready composition. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the crop (tight head-and-shoulders vs. waist-up), the eyeline, and where to leave clean space at the top for a masthead and cover lines. THEN GENERATE: frame the subject head-on with a confident, magnetic gaze into the lens; light the face with a soft beauty dish slightly above and a subtle fill so skin reads luminous with real pores and fine texture (not airbrushed plastic); set them against a seamless [BACKDROP COLOR] backdrop; shoot at roughly 85mm with a wide aperture for a flattering shallow depth and creamy fall-off; carry the [MOOD] through expression and tonal grade; keep clean negative space above the head. CONSTRAINTS: realistic skin with visible pores and natural catchlights in the eyes, accurate five-finger hands if hands appear, no warped jewelry or melted fabric, no extra teeth, no added text or logos in the image, one subject only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cover-portrait image only in a 4:5 vertical, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the backdrop color or tighten the crop.

Title: High-Fashion Studio Editorial On Seamless Color
Teaser: The bold, art-directed studio shot you see in fashion spreads — a full look on a saturated seamless background, sculpted hard-then-soft light, an expressive pose and deliberate empty space for layout. Pure editorial drama, nothing accidental.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [OUTFIT], pick a [BACKDROP COLOR], and optionally the [POSE/ATTITUDE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a high-fashion studio photographer and art director producing a single hero frame for an editorial spread. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [OUTFIT] against a vivid [BACKDROP COLOR] seamless studio backdrop with a strong, intentional pose. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the body line that flatters the garment's silhouette, the light shaping (one sculpting key plus a hint of rim), and which side keeps clean negative space for type. THEN GENERATE: place the subject off-center with a striking [POSE/ATTITUDE] that shows the garment's cut and movement; light with a directional key that carves form, a gentle fill, and a subtle edge light separating subject from background; keep fabric drape, seams and color tack-sharp; shoot full or three-quarter length with a slightly wide-normal lens; grade for a bold, contemporary editorial palette. CONSTRAINTS: keep the garment's real shape, fabric and color believable; realistic anatomy and natural hands, no floating limbs, no melted textiles or warped seams, no added text or logos, one subject only, lighting that looks like real strobes (no flat AI glow). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished studio editorial image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the pose or the backdrop color.

Title: Street-Style Candid (Off-Duty Editorial)
Teaser: The paparazzi-meets-fashion-week look — a full outfit caught mid-stride on a real city street, motion in the walk, natural light, a softly blurred urban backdrop. The 'off-duty model' aesthetic that powers lookbooks and brand campaigns.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [OUTFIT], set the [CITY/STREET SETTING], and optionally the [TIME OF DAY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a street-style fashion photographer capturing off-duty looks outside the shows. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] wearing [OUTFIT] candidly mid-walk in [CITY/STREET SETTING] as if caught between appointments. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the walking direction and stride, the believable gesture (adjusting a sleeve, glancing aside), and the time of day that gives the most flattering directional light. THEN GENERATE: capture a full-length candid with natural motion in the step and clothing; place the subject sharp against a softly blurred [CITY/STREET SETTING] with real architectural and street detail; light with directional [TIME OF DAY] daylight and gentle shadows; shoot at a normal focal length with a moderately shallow depth so the background falls away; keep the outfit's fabrics, layers and footwear accurate and crisp; feel candid and unposed, not stiff. CONSTRAINTS: realistic anatomy, natural five-finger hands, believable five-toe feet if visible, accurate garment construction (no fused seams), legible-but-unbranded surroundings (no fake logos or text), one subject only, authentic daylight (no studio look). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished street-style image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the city setting or the time of day.

Title: Garment Flat-Lay Lookbook Page
Teaser: Lays a whole outfit out top-down on a clean surface — the jacket, the tee, the trousers, the shoes and a few accessories arranged like a styled outfit-of-the-day. The tidy, shoppable flat-lay every lookbook and e-commerce drop needs.
PromptHint: List the [GARMENTS] in the outfit, name any [ACCESSORIES], and optionally set the [SURFACE/TEXTURE] and [PALETTE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a fashion stylist and still-life photographer shooting an overhead outfit flat-lay for a lookbook. TASK: Arrange [GARMENTS] together with [ACCESSORIES] as one cohesive styled outfit, photographed straight from directly above. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the pieces lay as if a person were wearing them (top above bottoms, shoes below), the spacing, and how to balance color across the frame so it reads as an intentional look. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down on a flat [SURFACE/TEXTURE] surface; lay the garments naturally with soft, believable fabric folds and the accessories tucked in at tidy angles; light evenly and softly so shadows are gentle and consistent; keep every piece crisp, color-accurate within the [PALETTE], and well-separated with balanced negative space; make it feel like a complete, wearable outfit, not random clutter. CONSTRAINTS: keep each garment's real shape, fabric weight and color believable; true overhead angle with no perspective tilt; soft even light with no harsh hotspots; no overlapping mess, no added text or logos, no fake brand labels. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay image only in 4:5 (or 1:1 if it composes better), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface texture or swap a garment.

Title: Streetwear Hype Campaign Frame
Teaser: The gritty, high-contrast streetwear shot — oversized fits, a low confident pose, hard flash or moody concrete-and-neon backdrop. The drop-day campaign energy that sells hoodies, sneakers and caps to a hype audience.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [STREETWEAR FIT], pick the [SETTING] (e.g. parking garage, neon alley), and optionally the [LIGHT STYLE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a streetwear campaign photographer shooting a drop-day hero frame with attitude. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [STREETWEAR FIT] in [SETTING] with the raw, confident energy of a hype brand launch. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the stance that reads as effortless confidence, whether the fit is oversized or tailored, and the [LIGHT STYLE] (hard direct flash vs. moody ambient neon) that suits the mood. THEN GENERATE: place the subject with a grounded, low-key powerful pose that shows the silhouette and layering of the fit; set them against a textured urban [SETTING] (concrete, steel, neon, signage shapes) softly out of focus; light with the chosen [LIGHT STYLE] so fabric texture, drawstrings, prints and sneaker detail pop; shoot wide-normal with deliberate, slightly cinematic grading and rich contrast; keep the streetwear pieces sharp and accurate. CONSTRAINTS: keep garment construction, prints and sneaker shapes believable; realistic anatomy and natural hands; no fake brand logos or readable copyrighted marks, no added text; authentic light (flash or ambient, not flat AI glow); one subject only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished streetwear campaign image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the setting or switch the lighting style.

Title: Beauty Close-Up With Editorial Makeup
Teaser: The tight beauty frame fashion magazines run for cosmetics — flawless luminous skin, a bold or graphic makeup look, glossy lips and sharp lashes, lit so every detail of the artistry reads. The shot that sells the makeup, not the outfit.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [MAKEUP LOOK] (e.g. graphic liner, dewy nude, bold red lip), and optionally the [SKIN FINISH] and [BACKDROP COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a beauty editorial photographer shooting a tight cosmetics close-up for a magazine page. TASK: Render an extreme beauty close-up of [SUBJECT] wearing [MAKEUP LOOK], framed to showcase the makeup artistry. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the crop (full face vs. eyes-and-lips), the eyeline, and the light that flatters skin while revealing the makeup's color and finish. THEN GENERATE: fill the frame with a sharp, luminous beauty close-up; render skin with a [SKIN FINISH] (dewy or matte) showing real pores, soft peach fuzz and natural catchlights; make the [MAKEUP LOOK] precise and editorial — clean liner edges, defined lashes, true lip color, blended shadow; light with a soft beauty dish and subtle reflectors against a [BACKDROP COLOR] backdrop; shoot at a macro-portrait focal length with crisp focus on the eyes and a gentle fall-off. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal skin texture (no plastic smoothing), symmetrical natural eyes with correct lashes, accurate teeth if lips part, no smeared or melted makeup edges, no added text or logos, one face only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished beauty close-up image only in 4:5 (or 1:1), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the makeup look or the skin finish.

Title: Accessory Hero On Model (Jewelry / Bag / Eyewear)
Teaser: Crops in on the accessory and makes it the star — a ring on a poised hand, a bag held just so, sunglasses catching the light — with the model present but supporting. The product-meets-editorial shot that sells the small, high-margin pieces.
PromptHint: Name the [ACCESSORY], describe the [SUBJECT/BODY PART] wearing it (e.g. hand, ear, face), and optionally the [MOOD] and [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an accessory and jewelry photographer shooting an on-model hero frame for an editorial campaign. TASK: Render [ACCESSORY] worn on [SUBJECT/BODY PART] so the accessory is the unmistakable focal point with the model supporting it. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the crop that hero's the piece (close on the hand, ear, neckline or face), the angle that flatters its shape, and the light that makes metal, stones or lenses catch and sparkle. THEN GENERATE: compose tight around the [ACCESSORY] worn naturally on [SUBJECT/BODY PART] with an elegant, relaxed pose; render the piece tack-sharp with accurate materials — true metal tone, faceted gem highlights, lens reflections or leather grain — while skin and surroundings fall softly out of focus; light with controlled directional light plus a small specular highlight to make the accessory gleam; set a clean [BACKDROP] and carry the [MOOD] through grade and styling. CONSTRAINTS: keep the accessory's real shape, material and proportions accurate; realistic skin and natural five-finger hands if a hand appears, correct ear/face anatomy; no warped metal, no extra fingers, no added text or logos; the accessory stays the clear hero. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished on-model accessory image only in 4:5, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the crop or the backdrop.

Title: Cinematic Mood Editorial With Atmosphere
Teaser: The moody, story-driven fashion frame — a model in a fog-lit warehouse, a sunset rooftop or a rain-streaked window, with film grain, color grading and real atmosphere. The dramatic editorial that feels like a still from a film.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [OUTFIT], set the [SCENE/LOCATION], and optionally the [MOOD] and [TIME/LIGHT].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a cinematic fashion photographer shooting an atmospheric narrative editorial. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [OUTFIT] within [SCENE/LOCATION] as a moody, film-still-like fashion frame. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single emotion the frame should convey, where the subject sits in the environment, and how light and atmosphere (fog, haze, rim light, window glow) carry the [MOOD]. THEN GENERATE: place the subject within a rich, evocative [SCENE/LOCATION] with real environmental detail and depth; introduce believable atmosphere — soft haze, [TIME/LIGHT] streaming in, gentle shadows — that wraps the figure; shoot with a cinematic aspect and a moderate telephoto for compression; grade with a cohesive filmic palette and a hint of grain; keep the outfit readable and sharp while the world has texture and falloff; pose the subject naturally within the story, not posed to camera. CONSTRAINTS: keep garments accurate; realistic anatomy and natural hands; atmosphere that looks physically real (no flat fog stickers); no added text or logos, no fake brand marks; one subject only; cohesive, intentional color grade. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished cinematic editorial image only in 3:2 (or 16:9), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the location or shift the mood.

Title: Mannequin / Ghost-Mannequin Product Cutout
Teaser: The clean e-commerce garment shot — the clothing photographed as if worn by an invisible body, holding its shape with no model, on a pure backdrop ready to cut out. The catalog workhorse for online stores and lookbook grids.
PromptHint: Name the [GARMENT], set the [BACKGROUND] (e.g. pure white), and optionally the [VIEW] (front/back) and [FABRIC NOTES].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an e-commerce apparel photographer producing a clean ghost-mannequin product image. TASK: Render [GARMENT] as if worn by an invisible body, holding a natural three-dimensional shape with no visible model, on a [BACKGROUND]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the [VIEW] (front or back), how the garment should fill out at shoulders, chest and hem, and where the inner collar or lining shows. THEN GENERATE: shoot the garment straight-on with the ghost-mannequin effect so it looks worn and filled (rounded shoulders, natural sleeve drape, structured hem) but the body is invisible; keep the [FABRIC NOTES] true — weave, sheen, stitching and color accurate; show the inner back collar where appropriate for a finished catalog look; light evenly and softly on a clean [BACKGROUND] with a faint, realistic contact shadow; keep edges crisp and easy to cut out. CONSTRAINTS: garment shape, fabric and color must stay accurate; no visible mannequin, hands or body parts; even shadow-free-but-grounded lighting; no warped seams or melted fabric; no added text or logos beyond what is genuinely on the garment. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished ghost-mannequin image only in 4:5 (or 1:1), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to switch to the back view or change the background.

Title: Full-Length Lookbook On-Model Studio Shot
Teaser: The clean head-to-toe catalog frame — full outfit, full body, even light, a relaxed natural pose on a neutral backdrop. The dependable lookbook and e-commerce shot that shows exactly how a look fits, with no distractions.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the full [OUTFIT], set the [BACKDROP TONE], and optionally the [POSE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a lookbook photographer shooting clean full-length on-model frames for a fashion catalog. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] head-to-toe in the complete [OUTFIT] against a neutral [BACKDROP TONE], styled to show exactly how the look fits and falls. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide a relaxed [POSE] that keeps the full silhouette visible, the framing that includes the shoes with breathing room top and bottom, and even light that shows true fabric color. THEN GENERATE: frame the subject full-length, centered with clean margin above the head and below the feet; pose them in a natural, easy stance that reveals the garment's cut, layering and drape; light with soft, even studio light and a gentle floor shadow for grounding; keep fabrics, footwear and color accurate and sharp from head to toe; set against a smooth [BACKDROP TONE] seamless. CONSTRAINTS: realistic anatomy, natural five-finger hands, correct footwear shape; accurate garment construction and color; no cropped feet, no warped proportions, no added text or logos; one subject only; clean catalog lighting (no heavy mood grade). OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished full-length lookbook image only in 4:5 (or 2:3), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 2:3 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the pose or the backdrop tone.

Title: Avant-Garde Conceptual Fashion Editorial
Teaser: The boundary-pushing art-fashion frame — sculptural silhouettes, surreal styling, an unexpected set and bold color, the kind of image that wins editorial awards. For when you want fashion as art, not a catalog.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [CONCEPT/OUTFIT], set the [SURREAL SETTING], and optionally the [COLOR STORY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an avant-garde fashion art director creating a conceptual editorial that treats clothing as sculpture. TASK: Render [SUBJECT] in [CONCEPT/OUTFIT] inside [SURREAL SETTING] as a bold, boundary-pushing art-fashion image. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the one striking idea the image is built around, how the silhouette and set reinforce it, and how the [COLOR STORY] ties the surreal elements together without becoming chaotic. THEN GENERATE: pose the subject in a sculptural, expressive shape that emphasizes the garment's form and the concept; build a surreal yet cohesive [SURREAL SETTING] with intentional props, scale play or unexpected materials; commit fully to the [COLOR STORY] across set, light and styling; light dramatically to model the silhouette and create depth; keep the image deliberate and gallery-worthy — strange but composed, never messy. CONSTRAINTS: even when surreal, keep human anatomy plausible and hands natural; garments should read as designed pieces, not noise; one cohesive concept (no random clutter); no added text or logos, no fake brand marks; intentional, balanced composition. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished conceptual editorial image only in 4:5 (or 3:2), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 250'), then one sentence I can paste back to push the concept further or change the color story.

Title: Vintage Film-Era Fashion Editorial
Teaser: Recreates a specific decade's fashion look — '70s warm grain, '90s desaturated minimalism, glossy '50s glamour — with period-true styling, color and film texture. The retro editorial for nostalgic campaigns and throwback drops.
PromptHint: Describe the [SUBJECT], name the [ERA] (e.g. 1970s, 1990s), name the [OUTFIT], and optionally the [FILM STOCK/GRAIN].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a fashion photographer recreating the look of a specific film era for a retro editorial. TASK: Photograph [SUBJECT] in [OUTFIT] styled and graded to feel authentically of the [ERA], as if shot on period film. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): recall the visual signatures of the [ERA] — the color palette, the hair and styling, the typical lens and grain — and decide how to make the frame read unmistakably as that decade. THEN GENERATE: style hair, makeup and outfit true to the [ERA]; pose the subject in a manner typical of that period's editorials; light and grade to match the decade's signature palette and contrast; emulate the chosen [FILM STOCK/GRAIN] with realistic grain, slight halation and period color shift; choose a focal length and depth consistent with the era's cameras; keep the outfit accurate while the whole frame feels like a found vintage scan. CONSTRAINTS: keep period styling believable and not a cartoon of the decade; realistic anatomy and natural hands; authentic film texture (grain and color, not a heavy filter overlay); no modern logos or anachronistic items; no added text; one subject only. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished vintage editorial image only in 4:5 (or 3:2), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the era or the film stock.

Title: Accessory Flat-Lay Detail Spread
Teaser: Arranges the small pieces — watches, sunglasses, rings, a belt, a fragrance — top-down on a textured surface with raking light that flatters metal, glass and leather. The luxe still-life flat-lay that makes accessories feel collectible and premium.
PromptHint: List the [ACCESSORIES], set the [SURFACE/TEXTURE], and optionally the [PALETTE] and [LIGHT MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a luxury still-life photographer shooting an overhead accessory flat-lay for an editorial detail page. TASK: Arrange [ACCESSORIES] in a refined top-down composition that makes each piece feel premium and collectible. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which piece anchors the frame, how to group the rest by size and material, and the spacing that feels editorial rather than crowded. THEN GENERATE: shoot perfectly top-down on a tactile [SURFACE/TEXTURE]; arrange the accessories with deliberate, breathing-room spacing and a clear hero; render materials accurately — gleaming metal, true glass reflections, fine leather grain, fragrance-bottle clarity; light with soft raking light that carves out texture and adds small specular highlights to metal and glass; keep the [PALETTE] cohesive and the [LIGHT MOOD] consistent; balance negative space so the eye moves through the pieces. CONSTRAINTS: keep each item's real shape, material and proportions accurate; true overhead angle with no perspective tilt; believable reflections and shadows (no fake glow); no overlapping clutter, no added text or logos, no invented brand marks. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished accessory flat-lay image only in 4:5 (or 1:1), then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the surface texture or swap an accessory.
